papers.title: Claude Computer Use: Let AI agents control desktops and browsers natively
papers.summary: Anthropic's computer use API lets Claude take screenshots, click, type, and scroll to complete tasks on real computers. Claude 3.7 Sonnet drives browsers and desktop apps end-to-end, enabling agents that operate software the way a human would. New improvements in tool call reliability and reduced hallucinated actions make it production-ready for browser automation pipelines.
papers.why_it_matters: Claude can now control any desktop app or browser autonomously, removing the need for custom UI automation scripts.
papers.what_it_is: The computer use API exposes screenshot, click, type, and scroll tools that let Claude operate real GUIs. Claude 3.7 Sonnet achieves state-of-the-art results on OSWorld and WebArena benchmarks. The API ships with a Docker reference implementation and a streaming live-view for monitoring agent actions.
papers.how_to_use_it: Pass `computer_use` as a beta flag when calling the Anthropic API, include screenshot and action tools in your tool list, then run the reference demo at github.com/anthropics/computer-use-demo.
papers.dev_take: Genuinely useful for automating legacy apps or browser workflows with no programmatic API — worth the extra latency cost for the right use case.
papers.detailed_summary:
**Why it matters**
Claude can now control any desktop app or browser autonomously, removing the need for custom UI automation scripts.

**What it is**
The computer use API exposes screenshot, click, type, and scroll tools that let Claude operate real GUIs. Claude 3.7 Sonnet achieves state-of-the-art results on OSWorld and WebArena benchmarks. The API ships with a Docker reference implementation and a streaming live-view for monitoring agent actions.

**How to use it**
Pass `computer_use` as a beta flag when calling the Anthropic API, include screenshot and action tools in your tool list, then run the reference demo at github.com/anthropics/computer-use-demo.

**Dev take**
Genuinely useful for automating legacy apps or browser workflows with no programmatic API — worth the extra latency cost for the right use case.

papers.title: Spring AI SDK for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is now Generally Available
papers.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is now generally available with the Spring AI SDK. This release includes support for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, a fully managed service for building and deploying AI agents at scale. Developers can use the Spring AI SDK to build AI agents that leverage Amazon Bedrock's foundation models and tools.

papers.title: Introspective Diffusion Language Models
papers.summary: We introduce Introspective Diffusion Language Models (IDLMs), a new class of diffusion language models that can introspect their own generation process.
